title: [InstCombine][AMDGPU] Disable PtrReplacer when select has mismatch AS
summary: The developers generalized an optimization by extending `PointerReplacer` to handle `select` instructions where only one operand is an `addrspacecast`. Initially, the transformation required both `select` operands to be `addrspacecast` from the same source address space. The patch introduces tracking via `NumValidAscFound` to count valid `addrspacecast` operands per `select`. If only one operand is an `addrspacecast`, the transformation is disallowed to preserve correctness. Additionally, the recursion now propagates the current `AddrSpaceCastInst` (`ASC`) to validate that chained casts maintain consistent address spaces across `select` and GEP users. This ensures the optimization applies safely to broader patterns where `addrspacecast` may appear on only one path of a `select`, while rejecting illegal cases. The approach demonstrates how to incrementally relax pattern constraints by adding stateful validation during recursive traversal.
